channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: NVIDIA vs AMD: Which AI Stock Offers Better Value and Growth for Investors in 2026
item.description: In 2026, NVIDIA and AMD compete fiercely in the AI chip market. NVIDIA leads in AI training, while AMD gains in inference workloads. Both face challenges and opportunities in a maturing AI landscape. [Nvidia, AMD]

item.title: A little-known Indian stock’s 530% rally shows hidden AI winners
item.description: Indian industrial companies are experiencing significant growth by supplying components for data centers. These firms are crucial for building and powering the infrastructure needed for artificial intelligence. Major global tech companies are investing billio… [Intel]

item.title: Amazon launches next-generation Proteus robot as part of €10 billion Europe investment
item.description: The launch forms part of the company’s €10 billion investment in its European fulfilment network, alongside broader expansion in robotics, AI-powered logistics, grocery delivery and its Alexa+ assistant. [Amazon]

item.title: AirTrunk plans $30 billion India data centre expansion
item.description: The proposed investment programme, which is backed by Blackstone and the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board, ranks among the largest digital infrastructure initiatives currently under consideration in India

item.title: 10 Strong Reasons Investors Are Still Buying NVIDIA Stock in 2026
item.description: NVIDIA continues to lead in AI infrastructure with strong market share, revenue growth, and innovation, making it a key investment for long-term growth in 2026. [Nvidia]

item.title: Companies are now cutting worker pay to fund AI investment- Teradata, TTEC slash employee benefits as AI spending surge
item.description: Teradata has frozen annual salary raises and TTEC has paused retirement benefits to fund AI spending, Business Insider reports. Experts warn more companies could follow. [ServiceNow]

item.title: Intel vs TSMC: Which Semiconductor Giant Offers Stronger Outlook for Investors in 2026
item.description: Intel and TSMC are key players in the AI semiconductor boom, with distinct strategies and market positions. Intel's turnaround offers high-risk potential, while TSMC provides stability and growth. [TSMC, Intel]

item.title: Top 5 AI Stocks Poised for Growth in USA Market as 2026 Momentum Builds
item.description: The AI sector is booming, with major U.S. companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Alphabet leading the charge. Analysts highlight their roles in AI infrastructure and applications, driving investor interest. [Alphabet, Microsoft, Nvidia]

item.title: Cloud ITSM Market Size by Solution Type, Deployment Mode - Global Forecast to 2031
item.description: The global cloud ITSM market is projected to grow from USD 11.09 billion in 2025 to USD 23.04 billion by 2031, at a CAGR of 13.0%. Key drivers include IT process automation, hybrid workforce enablement, and the demand for standardized service management frame…

item.title: India’s AI gap keeps global investors away, but valuations are turning attractive: Punita Kumar Sinha
item.description: India is currently sidelined from the global AI investment boom due to its lack of semiconductor manufacturing. While the Indian IT sector has corrected, market veteran Punita Kumar Sinha believes value is emerging, with domestic themes and earnings growth of…

item.title: Why 95% of enterprise GPUs sit idle while AI startups can't get compute
item.description: Enterprise GPU hoarding leaves startups starved for affordable AI compute despite massive idle capacity.
